title: Reading and Use of English – Part 2
description: For questions 9–16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only ONE word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: In the sunless depths of the ocean, where sunlight cannot reach, many creatures have evolved the ability to produce their own light, (0) a phenomenon known as bioluminescence.||This biological light is produced (9) by a chemical reaction between a light-emitting molecule and an enzyme. (10) Although it may seem like a rare occurrence to us, it is actually quite common in the deep sea. It serves a variety of purposes, (11) such as attracting prey, finding mates, or deterring predators. Some fish use light to blend (12) in with the faint glow from the surface, making them nearly invisible to hunters below. (13) Not only is this light used for survival, but it also allows for complex communication in the dark. (14) Despite the immense pressure and freezing temperatures, these organisms thrive in an environment (15) that would be fatal to most life forms. Scientists are only just beginning to understand the full extent of (16) how bioluminescence impacts the marine ecosystem. This mysterious world continues to provide us with new insights into the adaptability of life on our planet. Every deep-sea expedition uncovers more fascinating details about these glowing inhabitants, reminding us how little we actually know now.

example.text: In the sunless depths of the ocean, where sunlight cannot reach, many creatures have evolved the ability to produce their own light, (0) a phenomenon known as bioluminescence.
example.explanation: Indefinite article used to introduce a noun phrase in apposition.

questions.explanation: Preposition used to indicate the means or method of production.
Conjunction used to introduce a concessive clause.
Part of the fixed expression 'such as' used to provide examples.
Part of the phrasal verb 'blend in', meaning to look similar to the surroundings.
Part of the correlative conjunction 'Not only... but also'.
Preposition used to indicate contrast or concession.
Relative pronoun introducing a defining relative clause.
Wh-word used to introduce a noun clause describing a process or manner.
